testingStrategy.notes: Excellent test coverage - unit, integration, load, manual

rollbackStrategy.notes: Outstanding - best-in-class with 4 rollback levels and 10 triggers

security.notes: Good but missing DoS protection (resume attempt limits)

operationalReadiness.notes: Missing migration strategy for production deployment

userRequirements.notes: All requirements met, gaps in operational safety

critical.title: Missing Resume Attempt Limits
critical.description: No limit on resume retry attempts. Failed session could be retried indefinitely, causing resource exhaustion and DoS vulnerability.
critical.impact: Resource exhaustion, spam vulnerability, potential infinite loop
critical.evidence: Phase 4 has resumingSessions Set for in-memory dedup only, no persistent attempt tracking, no maximum attempt limit
critical.recommendation: Add resume_attempts column to schema, add attempt checking logic to orchestrator, max 3 attempts per session then permanent disable

critical.title: Missing Database Migration Strategy
critical.description: No migration strategy for production deployment. pending_messages table must be added to existing databases safely.
critical.impact: Deployment failure, data inconsistency, production downtime, no rollback migration
critical.evidence: Todo item mentions migration script but no implementation details, no migration runner, no version tracking, no execution procedure
critical.recommendation: Create migration system (hub/src/migrations/), implement runMigrations() and rollbackMigration(), add migration tracking table, document execution procedure

critical.title: Missing Offline Queue Persistence
critical.description: No handling for user closing browser during resume. Messages queued server-side but user doesn't know outcome if they leave.
critical.impact: User data loss confusion, poor UX, support burden (users ask 'did my message send?')
critical.evidence: Phase 5 shows 'Poll or wait for resume completion' but no offline handling, no localStorage queue, no handling for tab close/navigate away
critical.recommendation: Choose Option A (server-side only, simpler) or Option B (localStorage queue, better UX). Recommended: Option A for MVP

medium.title: Missing Queue Depth Monitoring Thresholds
medium.description: Queue limit (100) defined but no proactive monitoring or alerting before hitting limit. Only acts when overflow happens.
medium.impact: Reactive instead of proactive monitoring, no warning at 50/75 messages
medium.evidence: Line 25: 'Limit queue size per session (100 messages max)', Line 26: 'Provide queue depth metrics' but no alert thresholds
medium.recommendation: Add warning threshold at 50 messages, critical at 75 messages, emit queue status metric, add alerting rules

medium.title: Missing Test Data Cleanup Automation
medium.description: Tests mention 'Clean up test data' but no automated cleanup strategy defined.
medium.impact: Test database bloat, test pollution, slow test execution, flaky tests
medium.evidence: Line 186 says 'Clean up test data' but no implementation, no afterEach hooks, no test database isolation
medium.recommendation: Add afterEach/afterAll cleanup hooks, use test database isolation (:memory:), OR cleanup by test data prefix

strengths: Exceptional research foundation with two comprehensive reports
Outstanding rollback strategy - 4 levels, 10 triggers, 3 in-progress strategies
Feature flag implementation with gradual rollout (0-100%)
Comprehensive testing strategy - unit, integration, race conditions, performance, load
Security considerations - input validation, SQL injection prevention, rate limiting, auth
User requirements alignment - all requirements met
Clean data flow - no race conditions after previous fixes
Error handling coverage - 5 failure modes addressed

phaseAnalysis.title: Message Queue Service
phaseAnalysis.readiness: Ready, M1 can be addressed during implementation

phaseAnalysis.title: Testing & Validation
phaseAnalysis.readiness: Ready, M2 can be addressed during implementation

phaseAnalysis.title: Monitoring & Rollback
phaseAnalysis.readiness: Exemplary, use as template for other plans

architectureAssessment.dataFlowNotes: Flow is correct and safe. No race conditions. 202 response prevents handler execution on inactive session.
securityCoverage: MISSING: DoS protection (C1 - resume attempt limits)

riskBreakdown.justification: Core guard modification (mitigated by feature flag)

userRequirementsAlignment.evidence: No time-based exclusion in any phase

userRequirementsAlignment.requirement: Auto-resume on message to inactive session

compliance.notes: Compliant with workflow, code standards, and documentation requirements. Operational readiness gaps: migration strategy (C2), DoS protection (C1)

tasks: Update Phase 1: Add resume_attempts column to schema
Update Phase 4: Add attempt checking logic to orchestrator
Add test case: 'max resume attempts exceeded'
RECOMMENDED: Choose Option A (server-side only, simpler)
Update Phase 5: Remove offline persistence requirement
Update Phase 5: Document server-side queue behavior
Add success criterion: 'If user closes tab, messages in history'
OR: If Option B chosen, implement localStorage queue (4 hours)
OR cleanup by test data prefix

steps: Deploy code with feature flag OFF
Verify: Table created, feature flag off, existing behavior unchanged
Monitor: No errors, latency baseline recorded
Monitor: Resume failure rate < 10%, queue depth < 1000, latency +200ms
Check: No critical alerts, no user complaints
Decision: If metrics bad → rollback to 0%, if metrics good → proceed
Load test: 100 concurrent users to inactive sessions
Decision: If issues → rollback to 10%, if good → proceed

deploymentStrategy.rollbackTrigger: If any rollback trigger fires (see Phase 7), execute appropriate rollback level (1-4), investigate root cause, fix and redeploy

newIssuesIdentified: C1: Resume attempt limits - Not mentioned in PLAN_FIXES_SUMMARY.md
C2: Migration strategy - Not mentioned in PLAN_FIXES_SUMMARY.md
C3: Offline queue persistence - Not mentioned in PLAN_FIXES_SUMMARY.md
comparisonWithPreviousReview.whyNewIssuesFound: Previous review focused on race conditions and frontend/backend alignment. This review focused on operational safety, security, and production deployment. Different review perspective → different issues identified.

recommendation: Address C1, C2, C3 immediately (6-10 hours), then proceed with implementation (4 weeks). The plan demonstrates mature understanding of distributed systems challenges and has exceptional rollback strategy. Final Status: NEEDS_REVISION ⚠️ (3 critical issues)
